mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 9.5% triggers a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 92.5 triggers a demand shock that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 600,000 triggers a supply shock that increases housing inventory and reduces construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate and a rise in the CPI above 217.2 trigger a monetary and inflation shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in monetary and inflation shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 9.5% and a decline in industrial production below 92.5 trigger a labor market downturn and demand shock that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market downturn and demand shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 13,238.6 triggers a demand shock that reduces corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 600,000 and a decline in industrial production below 92.5 trigger a supply shock and demand shock that increases housing inventory and reduces construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply shock and demand shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
